seoTitle: How to Use Templates in Brevo Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to find, customize, and deploy saved or pre-made email templates in Brevo to speed up your newsletter and campaign workflow.
intro: Rebuilding your email layout from scratch every time you send a campaign wastes hours you don't have. This guide shows how to navigate Brevo's template library, customize an existing design with fresh text and images, and roll it straight into a live campaign. It's built for anyone who wants consistent branding without repetitive manual work.

steps.heading: Open the Templates dashboard
steps.body: From Campaigns in the left sidebar, expand the menu and click into the Templates section to see your saved designs and Brevo's pre-made layouts.

steps.heading: Choose a template
steps.body: Browse My Templates for your own saved designs, or check the free layouts tab, then click Use template on the one you want.

steps.heading: Confirm setup details
steps.body: Review your tracking name, sender email, and subject line before clicking Next step to load the template into the design workspace.

steps.heading: Customize the content
steps.body: Double-click text elements to update messaging, and swap image blocks for fresh media from your brand library.

steps.heading: Check mobile view and move to sending
steps.body: Use View Options to confirm the layout looks right on smartphones, then continue to select your recipient list and finalize delivery.

faq.question: Can I save my own custom templates in Brevo?
faq.answer: Yes, any layout you build can be saved to My Templates so you can reuse it for future campaigns without rebuilding it.

faq.question: Are Brevo's pre-made templates free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, the pre-made layout library is available inside the Templates tab alongside your own saved designs.

faq.question: Can I use a template for automated transactional emails?
faq.answer: Yes, templates can be set up as reusable layouts for automation or API-triggered messages, not just manual campaigns.

faq.question: What happens if my template looks broken on mobile?
faq.answer: Use the View Options toggle in the design workspace to preview mobile rendering and adjust block spacing before sending.
